name: Pane
description:
Library component. Models a tmux pane as the substrate that hosts the LLM session. The pane is what survives the agent's death: t.RespawnPane (internal/cmd/handoff.go:591) is the atomic kill-and-start primitive. Before the respawn, handoff sets `remain-on-exit` so the pane survives process death (handoff.go:571), then ClearHistory clears scrollback (handoff.go:576), then RespawnPane kills the current process and starts a fresh one with the rebuilt restart command (built with --continue so the new Claude resumes the conversation).

v0.2 (defense-doc P2.4): the pane now loops AlivePostRespawn -> Alive so subsequent cycles can respawn again. AlivePostRespawn becomes the steady state between cycles; Alive is reached briefly at t=0 and again after each cycle's respawn settles.

RESPAWN_REQUEST.description: Handoff cycle requests a respawn. Real: t.RespawnPane(pane, restartCmd).

BOOT_SUCCESSOR.description: After the respawn, the new pane process starts. Modeled as boot signal to the (successor) Agent. Real: tmux runs the restart command in the freshly killed pane; the new Claude session starts and SessionStart hook fires.

actors.name: Pane
actors.owner: Tmux pane lifecycle around handoff. Alive (predecessor running) -> Respawning (killing predecessor, starting successor) -> AlivePostRespawn (successor running) -> Alive (loop, ready for next cycle's respawn).

triples.event: tmux respawn-pane: predecessor process killed, successor process started fresh
pane settles into running state, ready for next cycle's respawn (loop)
